message:
fix(string,wchar): strdup/strndup/wcsdup use libc::malloc (bd-zgifl partial)

## Continuing the bd-dqqh1/bd-zgifl cross-allocator-free sweep

Three direct dup-family functions audited and fixed against the same
root cause as bd-dqqh1's host2netname:

- string_abi::strdup  (line 3484)
- string_abi::strndup (line 3588)
- wchar_abi::wcsdup   (line 1315)

All three were allocating via `crate::malloc_abi::raw_alloc` and
documenting "caller must free with free()". raw_alloc routes through
native_libc_malloc which, under NATIVE_MALLOC_REENTRY contention,
falls back to a static BUMP_HEAP — pointers outside any glibc arena
that glibc's free() cannot validate ("free(): invalid size" abort).

Switching all three to libc::malloc resolves the inconsistency:
- Under LD_PRELOAD: libc::malloc IS our own interposed malloc
  symbol, identical machinery, alloc/free pair matches.
- Under non-preload (debug test builds): libc::malloc is glibc's
  malloc, pairs with glibc's libc::free.

In both cases the caller's free() is now consistent with the
allocator that produced the buffer.

## Validation

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test string_abi_test  -> 124 passed, 0 failed
cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test wchar_abi_test   -> 106 passed, 0 failed
cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test     -> 35 passed, 0 failed
                                                          (regression check on bd-dqqh1 fix)

## Remaining bd-zgifl scope

~27 more raw_alloc sites in inet_abi, dirent_abi, stdlib_abi,
remaining rpc_abi, unistd_abi (13 sites) need the same audit. Each
fix is a 1-line edit + verification. Tracked in bd-zgifl's
per-callsite list.

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
